:::{#def-ph-risk-set-score}
#### Risk-set score total

The **risk-set score total** at event time $t_i$ is the sum of the
subject-specific hazard ratios over everyone at risk at $t_i$:

$$
S_i \eqdef \sum_{k \in R(t_i)} \red{\hazfactor(\vx_k)},
$$ {#eq-ph-risk-set-score}

where $R(t_i)$ is the risk set at $t_i$
and $\red{\hazfactor(\vx)} \eqdef \expf{\tp{\vx}\vb}$ is the hazard ratio (risk score) for covariate pattern $\vx$.
:::

:::{#exm-ph-risk-set-score}
#### Risk-set score total in the three-subject risk set

For the running scenario of @exm-ph-single-subj-fail —
$R(4) = \set{1, 2, 3}$ with hazard ratios
$\red{\hazfactor(\vx_1)} = 1$, $\red{\hazfactor(\vx_2)} = 2$, $\red{\hazfactor(\vx_3)} = 3$ —
the risk-set score total (@eq-ph-risk-set-score) is

$$
\ba
\red{S_i}
&= \red{\hazfactor(\vx_1)} + \red{\hazfactor(\vx_2)} + \red{\hazfactor(\vx_3)}
&& \text{(definition of } S_i \text{)}
\\
&= 1 + 2 + 3
&& \text{(substitute the hazard ratios)}
\\
&= 6 .
&& \text{(arithmetic)}
\ea
$$
:::
